rule.name: DENY_AUTHORITY_CLAIM
rule.note: ZERO fires — all designed cases masked by RESTRICTED_PATH

coverage_gap_analysis.summary:
SDO coverage audit of the Task 4.9d quality gate evidence reveals that the 40/40 (1.000) agreement score is structurally misleading. The DeterministicPolicyChecker absorbs 25 of 40 cases before the LLM classifies, leaving only 15 LLM-path cases. Of those 15: 12 ALLOW, 0 DENY, 3 ESCALATE. Zero adversarial cases reach the LLM. The 1.000 adversarial_security rate reflects checker determinism, not LLM robustness.
Five structural gaps identified:
coverage_gap_analysis.gap: All 22 DENY cases are pre-filtered. The LLM has never been tested on a DENY input. If a novel request bypasses all checker rules but is DENY-class, LLM behavior is unknown.

All 8 adversarial cases are pre-filtered. The adversarial_security metric measures checker quality, not LLM adversarial resistance. LLM robustness to role confusion, social engineering, and prompt injection is untested.

Rule 4 (authority-claim regex) has never fired. Cases 19 and 39 were designed to trigger it but both have /system/ paths that cause Rule 1 to fire first. If Rule 4's regex contains a bug, there is no evidence to detect it.
All 12 LLM ALLOW cases are simple LOW/MEDIUM operations on /home/user/ paths. No boundary ALLOW cases (near DENY/ESCALATE thresholds) test the LLM.
All 3 are clean structural matches. No ESCALATE edge cases exist in the LLM path.

scope_request.title: PA Quality Gate Corpus Hardening — LLM Classification Baseline
scope_request.rationale: Task 4.11 will modify the DeterministicPolicyChecker. The LLM classification baseline must be established on the CURRENT checker before those changes. If Task 4.11 runs first, we lose the ability to measure LLM-only performance against the production checker rules.

requirements.item: Minimum 10 LLM-path DENY cases (Category A — currently 0)
Minimum 5 LLM-path adversarial cases (Category B — currently 0)
Minimum 3 DENY_AUTHORITY_CLAIM validation cases on non-restricted paths (Category C)
Minimum 5 boundary ALLOW cases near DENY/ESCALATE thresholds (Category D)
All existing 40 cases preserved exactly (regression gate: 40/40)
No changes to services/, shared/, or launcher/ (test harness only)
